descriptions.studies: Studies are structured programs of learning designed to impart knowledge or skills in specific subjects or areas. They can range from academic disciplines and vocational training to professional development and personal interests. Studies are offered in various formats, including in-person classroom settings, online platforms, or hybrid arrangements.
descriptions.assistance: Accommodations are adjustments made to the environment or processes to enable students, employees, or participants with disabilities to participate fully. Examples include providing extra time on tests for students with learning disabilities, offering sign language interpreters for individuals who are deaf, adjusting work schedules for those with medical needs, or modifying a workspace for accessibility.
descriptions.socialBadges: Social Badges are digital icons or labels used on online platforms to signify achievements, statuses, roles, or milestones of users. They serve to recognize accomplishments, indicate member roles, encourage participation, and foster community engagement. These badges can denote anything from completing tasks, participating in events, to being part of specific groups.
descriptions.achievements: Achievements are milestones or accomplishments that are earned through effort, skill, or perseverance. They signify the completion of goals, the overcoming of challenges, or the recognition of excellence in various aspects of life, including personal, professional, educational, or recreational areas. Achievements can range from academic degrees, career advancements, personal milestones, creative endeavors, to competitive victories.
descriptions.portfolio: A portfolio is a curated collection of an individual's work, projects, or achievements that showcase their skills, experience, and growth over time. It serves as tangible proof of capability and creativity, often spanning professional, academic, or personal domains, and is used to demonstrate value to potential collaborators, employers, or audiences.
descriptions.skills: Skills are abilities or competencies developed through training, practice, or experience that enable an individual to perform tasks effectively. They can be classified into hard skills, which are specific, teachable, and often quantifiable abilities related to a particular task or job, such as coding, welding, or foreign language proficiency. Soft skills, on the other hand, are interpersonal and cognitive abilities that facilitate human interactions, such as communication, problem-solving, and teamwork.
descriptions.experiences: Experiences are the knowledge and skills gained from involvement in various professional roles and activities over time. They encompass the breadth of work-related tasks, projects, and responsibilities an individual has undertaken within different organizations or as a freelancer. Professional experiences contribute significantly to an individual's expertise, capabilities, and understanding of industry standards and practices.
descriptions.ids: IDs are essential digital or physical credentials used to verify an individual's identity in various contexts. They provide a secure way to store and access personal identification information, enabling access to services, facilities, or systems that require user authentication. Whether used for logging into platforms, verifying age, or gaining entry to secure areas, IDs are a fundamental tool in ensuring privacy, security, and convenience across different domains.
descriptions.families: Families are connected through shared learning journeys and accomplishments. This feature helps guardians organize profiles, manage permissions, and track educational progress. With tools for switching accounts, connecting games, and monitoring engagement styles, Families fosters growth and celebrates achievements while offering insights into each member's learning path.
descriptions.aiInsights: AI Insights are intelligent summaries generated from an individual's verified credentials, skills, learning experiences, and achievements. They provide a clear, holistic view of a learner's progress by analyzing authenticated data to identify strengths, highlight growth areas, and reveal meaningful patterns over time. These insights help learners understand their development, support educators in guiding their students, and enable more informed decisions around learning pathways and future opportunities.
descriptions.aiPathways: AI Pathways are personalized learning journeys generated by analyzing your verified skills, credentials, and career interests. They map out recommended courses, experiences, and milestones tailored to your goals, helping you discover new opportunities and build toward the career or learning outcomes that matter most to you.

chapi.title: About Chapi
chapi.p1: The Credential Handler API (CHAPI) is an open-source solution for communicating Verifiable Credentials on the Web.
chapi.p2: Think, trusted and confidential carrier pigeon, but for Verifiable Credentials.
chapi.p3: It allows your digital wallet to send or receive Verifiable Credentials from an independent third-party verifier or issuer in a way that establishes trust and preserves privacy.

iframe.intro: Create an embedded app that runs inside the LearnCard wallet. Your app can request user identity, send credentials, and more.
iframe.step1Desc: Create and host a website that can be embedded in an iframe. You'll need to configure CORS headers.
iframe.step1Important: Your server must include these response headers:
iframe.step2Desc: Install the LearnCard Partner Connect SDK to communicate with the wallet.
iframe.step3Desc: Set up the SDK in your application to communicate with the LearnCard wallet.
iframe.step4DescHasPerms: Based on your selected permissions, here are the methods you can use:
iframe.step4DescNoPerms: Select permissions above to see relevant API methods. Here are some common ones:
iframe.step4Tip: Select {{count}} permission(s). The methods above correspond to your selections.

consent.intro: Use the Consent Redirect flow to collect user consent and credentials from your external application. Users will be redirected to LearnCard to grant permissions, then back to your app with their credentials.
consent.step1Desc: First, create or select a Consent Flow Contract that defines what permissions your app needs. You can do this in the Developer Portal or via the API.
consent.step1Important: Copy and save your Contract URI — you will need it to send credentials later.
consent.step1NoContract: Select a consent flow contract above to see your URI here.
consent.step2Desc: Create an endpoint to handle the redirect from LearnCard. The user's DID and a VP JWT (containing a delegate credential) will be included in the URL parameters.
consent.step2Note: Store the did and vp (a VP JWT containing a delegate credential) to identify and send credentials to this user later.
consent.step3Desc: Generate an API key to authenticate your backend with the LearnCard Network.
consent.step4Desc: Install and initialize the LearnCard SDK in your backend application.
consent.step4Init: Then initialize with your API key:
consent.step5Desc: When you're ready to send a credential to a user, use the simplified send method. This handles credential creation, signing, and delivery in one call.
consent.step5CredAdded: Custom credential added to code below
consent.step5WhatsThis: Creates a credential template, issues it to the user, and writes it to your consent flow contract — all in one call.
consent.flowStep1: User clicks "Connect with LearnCard" in your app
consent.flowStep2: User is redirected to LearnCard to grant consent
consent.flowStep3: LearnCard redirects back to your app with their DID
consent.flowStep4: Your backend stores the user's DID
consent.flowStep5: When ready, your backend issues credentials to that DID
consent.step6Desc: As the contract owner, you can also query consent data and transactions:
consent.step6AllData: Get all consented data for your contract:
consent.step6UserData: Get consent data for a specific user:

server.intro: Server Headless integration uses the Universal Inbox API to issue credentials directly to users via email or phone — no wallet interaction required from your side.
server.step1Desc: A signing authority is required to sign credentials on your behalf.
server.step2Desc: Generate an API key to authenticate your backend with the LearnCard Network.
server.step3Desc: Install and initialize the LearnCard SDK in your backend application.
server.step3Init: Then initialize with your API key:
server.step4Desc: Send credentials to users by email or phone. LearnCard handles delivery automatically.
server.step4WhatsThis: If the recipient has a LearnCard wallet linked to that email, the credential is delivered instantly. Otherwise, they receive an email with a claim link.
server.step5Desc: Receive notifications when credentials are delivered or claimed.
server.step6Desc: You can also use the REST API directly without the SDK.
server.flowStep1: Your server calls inbox.issue with email + credential
server.flowStep2: LearnCard signs the credential with your signing authority
server.flowStep3: If user has wallet → delivered instantly to their inbox
server.flowStep4: If new user → email sent with magic link to claim
server.flowStep5: Webhook notifies you of delivery status

directLink.intro: Direct Link is the simplest integration — users click your app and are redirected to your URL with optional query parameters for user context.
directLink.step1Desc: Provide the URL where users will be redirected when they launch your app.
directLink.step1Note: This URL is configured in the Launch Configuration step.
directLink.step2Desc: LearnCard can append the user's DID as a query parameter for identification.
directLink.step3Desc: If you need to verify the user's identity, you can resolve their DID.
directLink.useCase1: Simple web apps that don't need wallet interaction
directLink.useCase3: Apps that only need user identification

publishableKeyStep.title: Your Publishable Key
publishableKeyStep.description: Use this publishable key to authenticate credential claims from your website. It's safe to expose in client-side code.
publishableKeyStep.statusReadyDesc: Your publishable key is ready to use
publishableKeyStep.statusWarningDesc: Select a project from the header dropdown
publishableKeyStep.keyHint: This key can only be used to claim credentials. Keep your secret key secure on your server.
publishableKeyStep.noProjectDesc: Select or create a project using the dropdown in the header to continue.

addTargetStep.title: Add HTML Target Element
addTargetStep.description: Add a container element to your page where the "Claim Credential" button will appear.
addTargetStep.whatGetsRenderedDesc: The SDK replaces the target element with a styled button. When clicked, it opens a modal for the user to verify their email and claim the credential.

loadSdkStep.title: Load the Embed SDK
loadSdkStep.description: Choose how to load the SDK in your project.
loadSdkStep.zeroDepsDesc: The SDK is a single optimized file (~15KB gzipped) with no external dependencies. It works on any website — no React, Vue, or framework required.

configureStep.title: Configure the SDK
configureStep.description: Create your credential templates and customize branding for the claim experience. Templates persist as reusable credentials that you can reference by URI.
configureStep.keyHint: Change your project using the dropdown in the header.
configureStep.noProjectDesc: Select or create a project using the dropdown in the header to continue.
configureStep.partnerNameHint: Shown alongside your logo in the claim modal. Not included on the issued credential.
configureStep.backgroundIssuanceDesc: Ask the user for permission to issue future credentials without requiring email verification each time.
configureStep.whitelistedDomainsDesc: Add the domains where you'll embed this claim button. The API will only accept claims from these domains.
configureStep.whitelistedDomainsEmpty: No domains whitelisted yet. The embed will not work until you add at least one domain.
configureStep.builderOpenWarning: Save or cancel the template you're editing before continuing.

testStep.title: Test Your Integration
testStep.description: Verify your configuration and preview the claim button your users will see.
testStep.completeChecks: Complete all pre-flight checks above to see a live preview of the claim button.
testStep.userFlowStep2Desc: A 6-digit code is sent to verify
testStep.userFlowStep3Desc: Credential is issued to their wallet
testStep.userFlowStep4Desc: LearnCard wallet opens, onSuccess is called
testStep.returningUsersDesc: The SDK remembers logged-in users via localStorage. On their next visit, they'll see an "Accept Credential" button instead of entering email/OTP again.
testStep.readyToGoLiveDesc: Users can now claim credentials directly from your website.

goLive.title: Ready to Embed!
goLive.description: You've set up everything needed to embed claim buttons on your website. Activate your integration to start accepting claims in production.

embedClaim.noIntegration: Please select an integration from the header dropdown to continue.

apiTokenStep.title: Create an API Token
apiTokenStep.description: Your server needs an API token to authenticate with LearnCard. This token should be kept secret and never exposed in client-side code.
apiTokenStep.statusReadyHint: Copy a token to use in your code
apiTokenStep.securityWarning: Never expose your API token in client-side code or commit it to version control.

signingAuthorityStep.title: Set Up Signing Authority
signingAuthorityStep.description: A signing authority cryptographically signs your credentials, making them verifiable. This proves the credentials actually came from you.
signingAuthorityStep.infoItem1: Creates a cryptographic key pair for signing
signingAuthorityStep.infoItem2: Registers the key with LearnCard's verification network
signingAuthorityStep.infoItem3: Allows anyone to verify credentials you issue

createTemplatesStep.title: Create Templates
createTemplatesStep.description: Design your credential templates using the visual builder. Each template becomes a reusable Boost that you can reference by URI when issuing credentials via API.
createTemplatesStep.builderOpenWarning: Save or cancel the template you're editing before continuing.

issueVerifyStep.title: Issue & Verify
issueVerifyStep.description: Use your template to issue credentials via API. Select a template and recipient, then run the generated code in your application.
issueVerifyStep.recipientDesc: Enter a recipient to see the send code. Supports email, DID, phone, or profile ID.
issueVerifyStep.checkCredentialsDesc: After running your code, click below to check if the credential was sent successfully.
issueVerifyStep.credentialSentToEmail: Credential sent successfully to email/phone recipient!
issueVerifyStep.credentialCheckFailed: No new credentials detected. Make sure you ran your code.
issueVerifyStep.credentialCheckError: Failed to check credentials. Please try again.
issueVerifyStep.noTokenWarning: Select an API token above to enable verification.

goLive.title: Ready to Issue Credentials!
goLive.description: You've set up everything needed to issue verifiable credentials via API. Activate your integration to start issuing in production.
goLive.completedItems0: Created API token for server-side access

issueCredentials.noIntegration: Please select an integration from the header dropdown to continue.

createContractStep.title: Create a Consent Flow Contract
createContractStep.description: A consent contract defines what data you're requesting and why. Users must accept the contract before sharing their data.
createContractStep.redirectFlowDescription: Consent Redirect Flow: Collect user consent and credentials from your external application. Users will be redirected to LearnCard to grant permissions, then back to your app with their credentials.
createContractStep.saveContractWarning: Important: Save this Contract URI — you'll need it to send credentials later.

redirectHandlerStep.title: Set Up Your Redirect Handler
redirectHandlerStep.description: When users click "Connect with LearnCard" in your app, they'll be redirected to LearnCard to grant consent, then back to your app with their DID and credentials.
redirectHandlerStep.callbackUrlHint: Users will be redirected here after granting consent
redirectHandlerStep.step1Desc: Create a button that redirects users to the consent flow:
redirectHandlerStep.step2Desc: Create an endpoint to handle the redirect. The user's DID and a VP JWT (containing a delegate credential) will be included in the URL parameters.
redirectHandlerStep.step2Hint: Store the <0>did</0> and <1>vp</1> (a VP JWT containing a delegate credential) to identify and send credentials to this user later.
redirectHandlerStep.flowStep1: User clicks "Connect with LearnCard" in your app
redirectHandlerStep.flowStep2: User is redirected to LearnCard to grant consent
redirectHandlerStep.flowStep3: LearnCard redirects back to your app with their DID
redirectHandlerStep.flowStep4: Your backend stores the user's DID
redirectHandlerStep.flowStep5: When ready, your backend issues credentials to that DID

apiSetupStep.title: Set Up Your Backend
apiSetupStep.description: Initialize the LearnCard SDK on your backend to send credentials and query consent data.
apiSetupStep.statusSelectHint: Select a token to use in your code
apiSetupStep.securityWarning: Never expose your API token in client-side code or commit it to version control.
apiSetupStep.step1Desc: Install the LearnCard SDK in your backend application:
apiSetupStep.step2Security: Security: Store your API token in environment variables, never commit it to code.
apiSetupStep.fetchError: Failed to load API tokens. Please try refreshing the page.

sendCredentialsStep.title: Create Credential Templates
sendCredentialsStep.description: Create credential templates that you'll issue to users who connect with your app. Templates are saved and reusable across multiple issuances.
sendCredentialsStep.step2Desc: Use the <0>send</0> method to issue credentials to users who have connected with your app:
sendCredentialsStep.step2Info: What this does: Issues the credential from your template to the user and writes it to your consent flow contract — all in one call.
sendCredentialsStep.step3Desc: As the contract owner, you can query consent data and transactions:
sendCredentialsStep.step3Sub1: Get all consented data for your contract:
sendCredentialsStep.step3Sub2: Get consent data for a specific user:
sendCredentialsStep.tip1: Store API keys in environment variables, never in code
sendCredentialsStep.tip2: Use <0>templateUri</0> to reference saved templates instead of inline credentials
sendCredentialsStep.tip3: Store user DIDs securely with their account data

testStep.title: Test Your Integration
testStep.description: Verify your consent flow works end-to-end before going live.
testStep.step1Desc: Click the button below to test the consent redirect with your actual contract and callback URL. You should be redirected to LearnCard to grant consent, then back to your callback URL.
testStep.missingConfigDesc: Missing configuration: Go back and ensure you've set both a contract URI (Step 1) and callback URL (Step 2).
testStep.step2Desc: After the user grants consent, LearnCard redirects them to your callback URL with these query parameters:
testStep.didDesc: The user's decentralized identifier (DID). Use this to send credentials to them.
testStep.vpDesc: A VP JWT containing a delegate credential. This proves the user authorized your app to act on their behalf for this contract.
testStep.exampleCallback: Example callback URL your server will receive:
testStep.step3Desc: After receiving the callback, run the send command from your backend to issue a credential:
testStep.step3Verify: Verify: The recipient should see the credential in their LearnCard wallet. Check the dashboard Connections tab to confirm delivery.

goLive.title: Ready to Connect!
goLive.description: You've set up everything needed for consent-based data sharing. Activate your integration to start connecting with users.

consentFlow.noIntegration: Please select an integration from the header dropdown to continue.

apiTokenStep.title: Create an API Token
apiTokenStep.description: You'll need an API token to authenticate webhook requests and make API calls from your server.

webhookEndpointStep.title: Create Your Webhook Endpoint
webhookEndpointStep.description: Create an HTTPS endpoint on your server that can receive POST requests from LearnCard.

handleEventsStep.title: Handle Events
handleEventsStep.description: LearnCard sends different event types. Handle each one based on your needs.
handleEventsStep.eventReceived: A credential was sent to a user
handleEventsStep.eventConsentGranted: User granted consent to a contract

testStep.title: Test Your Webhook
testStep.description: Make sure your webhook endpoint is working correctly before going live.
testStep.testSuccess: Test event sent! Check your server logs.
testStep.readyDescription: Your server can now receive real-time events from LearnCard.

understandStep.title: Understanding Verifiable Credentials
understandStep.description: Verifiable Credentials (VCs) are digital documents that prove something about a person — like a badge, certificate, or ID — that can be cryptographically verified.
understandStep.verifyBullet1: The credential hasn't been tampered with
understandStep.verifyBullet2: It was issued by who it claims

requestStep.title: Requesting Credentials
requestStep.description: You can request credentials from users in different ways depending on your integration type.
requestStep.option2Desc: Accept credentials posted directly to your API endpoint.

verifyStep.title: Verifying Credentials
verifyStep.description: Use the LearnCard SDK to cryptographically verify credentials. No API key required for verification.

testStep.title: Test Verification
testStep.description: Try verifying a credential right here. Paste a credential JSON below.
testStep.readyDescription: You can now accept and verify credentials from users.

courseCatalog.noIntegration: Please select an integration from the header dropdown to continue.

dashboard.title: Admin Dashboard
dashboard.chooseFromSidebar: Choose a listing from the sidebar

actions.rejectedMsg: This listing was rejected. Send it back as a draft for revision.

promotion.featuredCarouselDesc: Prominently displayed in the featured section
promotion.standardDesc: Normal visibility in search and browse
